items.headline: Zion Williamson Tweaks Ankle in Pelicans' Win
items.summary: New Orleans Pelicans star forward Zion Williamson left Saturday night's game against the Utah Jazz in the second quarter after tweaking his right ankle. The Pelicans still secured a 115-105 victory. Interim coach James Borrego hopes the injury is not serious, marking another setback for the oft-injured player who had already missed 16 games this season.

items.headline: Curry Still Out, Porzingis Sidelined with Illness for Warriors
items.summary: Stephen Curry missed his 10th consecutive game for the Golden State Warriors due to a right knee injury, with reports indicating he'll be out 'a little longer'. Adding to the Warriors' woes, newly acquired center Kristaps Porzingis also sat out Saturday's game against the Lakers due to illness.

items.headline: Garland and Zubac Nearing Returns, Avdija Remains Out
items.summary: Darius Garland is reportedly nearing his Los Angeles Clippers debut as early as March 1 after participating in a full 5-on-5 scrimmage, recovering from a hamstring injury. Ivica Zubac, recently traded to the Indiana Pacers, is also expected back around March 1 from an ankle injury. Meanwhile, Israeli forward Deni Avdija (POR) has been ruled out for his third straight game with a lower-back injury.

items.headline: Pistons Edge Cavaliers in Overtime Thriller
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons secured their second straight win, defeating the Cleveland Cavaliers 122-119 in overtime. Jalen Duren led the Pistons with a dominant double-double performance of 33 points and 16 rebounds.

items.headline: Celtics Dominate Nets in High-Scoring Affair
items.summary: The Boston Celtics put on an offensive clinic, beating the Brooklyn Nets 148-111.

items.headline: Knicks Take Down Bucks
items.summary: The New York Knicks defeated the Milwaukee Bucks 127-98. Jalen Brunson led the Knicks in scoring with 27 points.

items.headline: Grizzlies Snap Losing Streak Against Mavericks
items.summary: The Memphis Grizzlies ended their three-game losing streak by defeating the Dallas Mavericks 124-105. Cam Spencer was the top scorer for the Grizzlies with 25 points.

items.headline: Thunder Prevail Over Nuggets in Overtime Battle
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder beat the Denver Nuggets 127-121 in overtime, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander leading the charge with 36 points and 9 assists. Chet Holmgren added 15 points and 21 rebounds.

items.headline: Lakers Rout Short-Handed Warriors
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers snapped a three-game losing streak with a dominant 129-101 victory over the Golden State Warriors. Luka Doncic led with 26 points, 8 assists, and 6 rebounds, while LeBron James added 22 points, 9 assists, and 7 rebounds.

items.headline: Pelicans Extend Winning Streak Against Jazz
items.summary: The New Orleans Pelicans secured their fourth consecutive win, defeating the Utah Jazz 115-105. Saddiq Bey was the top scorer for the Pelicans with 24 points.

items.headline: Hornets Win Fourth Straight, Defeat Trail Blazers
items.summary: The Charlotte Hornets continued their winning ways, beating the Portland Trail Blazers 109-93 for their fourth straight victory. Brandon Miller scored 26 points and hit six 3-pointers.

items.headline: Raptors Down Wizards in High-Scoring Contest
items.summary: The Toronto Raptors defeated the Washington Wizards 134-125. Immanuel Quickley had a double-double with 27 points and 11 assists.

items.headline: New York Knicks vs. San Antonio Spurs
items.summary: The red-hot San Antonio Spurs, riding an 11-game winning streak, visit Madison Square Garden to face the New York Knicks.

items.headline: Immanuel Quickley's Double-Double Fuels Raptors
Rookie Brandon Miller Shines for Hornets
Saddiq Bey Leads Pelicans in Scoring
Rookie Kon Knueppel Among League's Top Three-Point Shooters
Lakers and Warriors Eyeing Trey Murphy III
items.summary: Rumors suggest the Los Angeles Lakers are targeting New Orleans Pelicans forward Trey Murphy III for the 2026 offseason to bolster their wing scoring and defense. The Golden State Warriors are also reportedly interested, setting up a potential bidding war for the $112 million superstar.

items.headline: NBA Buyout Market Remains Quiet Ahead of March 1 Deadline
items.summary: As the March 1 deadline for waiving players to preserve playoff eligibility approaches, the NBA buyout market has been relatively quiet. Khris Middleton and DeMar DeRozan are among the veteran names being discussed as potential buyout candidates.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Playoff Picture Tightens
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons lead the Eastern Conference with a 44-14 record, followed by the Boston Celtics (39-20) and New York Knicks (38-22). The Cleveland Cavaliers are currently fourth at 37-24.

items.headline: Western Conference Race Heating Up
items.summary: In the Western Conference, the Oklahoma City Thunder hold the top spot with a 46-15 record, with the San Antonio Spurs close behind at 43-16. The Houston Rockets (37-22) and Denver Nuggets (37-23) round out the top four.

items.headline: Ja Morant Still Out with Elbow Injury, Re-evaluation in Two Weeks
items.summary: Memphis Grizzlies star Ja Morant remains sidelined due to an elbow injury. The organization recently released an update stating he is progressing well and will be re-evaluated in two weeks.

items.headline: Jayson Tatum Expected Back Mid-March After Achilles Injury
items.summary: Boston Celtics forward Jayson Tatum, who has been dealing with a torn right Achilles tendon, is expected to return to action around March 10.
